Like, are you just going to advertise it as, hey, Stripe Atlas if you're signing up for us or like, how are you planning on doing that?

Yeah, so most customers that we have as a percentage of our revenue come directly to Discern looking for a new registered agent or a filing service, which we provide both in one unified product offering.
Stripe Atlas does formations for startups, and they actually do one in five Delaware C-Corp formations today, which is pretty crazy.
And if you need to foreign register after you form an entity, then Stripe sends you to us, like we're in their checklist.
After you form your entity, it's like, need to register in other states, go to Discern.
And so we've had that partnership for a few months.
It's not huge because we probably get 300 people to our website every month from that group, but they're the smallest possible customers.
One or two state registrations, they are very uneducated about the subject, which makes them high from a servicing cost perspective.
And they're...
They default very small businesses because they've literally just been formed.
So what we used to do was take sales calls for all these.
It was a good way to train account executives and whatever, but they're like literally $700 a year subscriptions.
Like it's tiny.
So we just turned on a self-service function in Stripe Atlas where you can click get started and it takes you right into our onboarding flow.
And so those people have access to our self-service onboarding, which nobody has ever had before.
We have not rolled that out to the rest of the site because we don't want to allow any random stranger to sign up for discern.
The main reason is we have unpredictable servicing costs on some customers.
And I mentioned statutory obligations for a registered agent.
If we become your registered agent, we have to deliver service of process.
If you get sued or a state notice, we have to deliver it.
We also have to do an OFAC check on the customer, see if they're on a watch list, essentially.
And then if you don't pay us for some reason, we have to pay to resign as a registered agent.
There's a filing you have to do to resign as a registered agent.
So for all of those reasons, it doesn't make sense necessarily for us today to open up self-service to everyone.
But as a result of how successful our self-service rollout's gone with Stripe, we're now thinking about it a lot more than we used to.
